response_text:
# Git Branching Strategies: GitFlow vs Trunk-Based Development

## Core Philosophy Difference

```
GitFlow: Multiple long-lived branches, structured releases
Trunk-Based: Single main branch, continuous integration
```

---

## GitFlow

### Branch Structure
```
main          ──●──────────────────────●──────────────●──
                │                      │              │
develop       ──●──●──●──●──●──●──●──●──●──●──●──●──●──
                   │        │     │
feature/login ─────●──●──●──┘     │
                              │   │
release/1.2   ────────────────●──●┘
                              │
hotfix/bug    ────────────────────●──●──┘
```

### Branch Types & Purposes

| Branch | Lifetime | Purpose |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `main` | Permanent | Production-ready code only |
| `develop` | Permanent | Integration branch |
| `feature/*` | Temporary | New features |
| `release/*` | Temporary | Release preparation |
| `hotfix/*` | Temporary | Emergency production fixes |

### Workflow Example
```bash
# Start a feature
git checkout develop
git checkout -b feature/user-authentication

# Work on feature...
git add .
git commit -m "Add login endpoint"
git commit -m "Add JWT validation"

# Merge back to develop
git checkout develop
git merge --no-ff feature/user-authentication
git branch -d feature/user-authentication

# Create release branch
git checkout -b release/1.2.0
# Bug fixes, version bumps only
git commit -m "Bump version to 1.2.0"

# Merge release to main AND develop
git checkout main
git merge --no-ff release/1.2.0
git tag -a v1.2.0

git checkout develop
git merge --no-ff release/1.2.0
git branch -d release/1.2.0

# Emergency hotfix
git checkout main
git checkout -b hotfix/critical-security-fix
git commit -m
